name: Ginger Ginger Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust a rack one-third up from the bottom of the oven and preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter a 10 x 4 1/4-inch angel food tube pan (or a single non-stick pan with an 18-cup capacity) and dust it thoroughly with fine, dry bread crumbs. Invert over paper and shake out excess crumbs; set aside.
3. Sift together the flour and baking soda in a bowl.
4. Cut candied ginger into 1/4 to 1/3-inch pieces and set aside.
5. Grate fresh ginger and set aside.
6. Beat butter until soft in a large mixing bowl. Gradually beat in grated fresh ginger and 2 1/4 cups of sugar (reserve the remaining half cup). Add egg yolks.
7. On low speed, gradually add the sifted dry ingredients in two additions, alternating with the sour cream in one addition. Remove the bowl from the mixer and stir in half of the diced candied ginger; set aside.
8. In a small mixing bowl, beat egg whites until they hold a soft shape. Gradually add the remaining half cup of sugar. Increase speed to beat only until stiff peaks form.
9. Fold the egg whites into the batter in three additions—fold partially with the first two and fold thoroughly with the last addition.
10. Turn batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top. Sprinkle reserved candied ginger on top.
11. Bake for about 1 hour, or until a cake tester inserted gently comes out clean (the cake will not rise to the top of the pan). Let cool in the pan for 10-15 minutes; it will sink about 1 inch as it cools.
12. Cover the pan with a cake plate and turn over carefully, removing the pan. Let cool completely.
This is a Maida Haetter recipe (a piece 3 to 4 inches long and about 1 inch thick).
